sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The left panel object is at cols 6-9, rows 16-19. This is the left side of the panel. The object is a 4x4 square-like shape made of 'c' (charcoal) cells on a 'G' (dark gray) background.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. Looking at the image:
- Left panel: gray background with a dark gray object at the left side
- Right panel: checkerboard with yellow objects
- Bottom: two small squares (blue on left, gray on right) and a blue blob
Wait, I need to look at the image more carefully. The image shows:
- Left panel upper: gray with a dark gray object
- Left panel lower: gray with button bars
- Left bottom: blue square with yellow/white pattern
- Right panel upper: checkerboard with yellow objects
- Right panel lower: white with button bars
- Right bottom: blue blob
The two small squares at the bottom (between the panels) are:
- Left: blue square with yellow/white pattern
- Right: gray square with yellow/white pattern
Wait, looking at the image again, I see two small squares at the very bottom, between the left and right panels. These are NOT the blue square and blue blob I was clicking before. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the ASCII output:
- Row 52: BBBBBBBBBBBgggggggggggBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBWWWWWWWWWWWWWBBBBB
- Row 53: BBBBBBBBBBBgBBBBBBBBBgBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBWBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB
The two small squares are:
- Left: rows 52-53, cols 11-21 (gray border, dark gray inside)
- Right: rows 52-53, cols 42-52 (white border, dark gray inside)
And below them:
- Left blue square: rows 54-62, cols 7-17
- Right blue blob: rows 54-62, cols 48-58
So the layout is:
- Left panel: cols 1-30
  - Upper: rows 1-30 (gray background with object)
  - Buttons: rows 32-50
  - Small square: rows 52-53, cols 11-21
  - Blue square: rows 54-62, cols 7-17
- Right panel: cols 32-61
  - Upper: rows 1-30 (checkerboard with yellow objects)
  - Buttons: rows 32-50
  - Small square: rows 52-53, cols 42-52
  - Blue blob: rows 54-62, cols 48-58
I clicked the left blue square at row 58, col 12 and the object moved. I clicked the right blue blob at row 58, col 50 and nothing changed (just an animation).
Let me try clicking the small squares at rows 52-53. These might be the actual interactive elements.
Actually wait, let me look at the image again. The image shows:
- Left bottom: a blue square with yellow/white dots inside
- Right bottom: a gray square with yellow/white dots inside
- Far right bottom: a blue blob
So there are THREE elements at the bottom:
1. Left blue square (with pattern)
2. Right gray square (with pattern)
3. Far right blue blob
But in the ASCII, I only see two squares at rows 52-53 and then the blue square/blob at rows 54-62. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the ASCII more carefully:
- Row 54: BBbbbbbbbbbBggg...bbbbbbbBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB
- The left blue square is at cols 7-17, rows 54-62
- The right blue blob is at cols 48-58, rows 54-62
And the small squares at rows 52-53 are:
- Left: cols 11-21, gray border
- Right: cols 42-52, white border
These small squares might be the "selectors" or "indicators" that show which panel is active.
Let me try clicking on the small squares to see if they do anything.

sections.content:

# Left small square: cols 11-21, rows 52-53
# Right small square: cols 42-52, rows 52-53

# First, let me try the left small square
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 52, 'col': 16}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
